get.description:
Aggregates per-cut waste area into a per-material report.
Waste cost is allocated using the originating board sheet's
cost-per-mm² (sheet_cost / sheet_area).

get.summary: Get costing record by work order
List costing adjustments for a work order

post.summary: Create a costing adjustment for a finalized work order

post.description: Computes (or re-computes) the costing record for a work order. ACTUAL costing on a COMPLETED WO requires at least one non-zero cost component (material/auxiliary/labor); otherwise returns 412 with the Vietnamese message "WO chưa có chi phí vật tư/nhân công, không thể tính giá thành". Estimated costing on PLANNED WOs is exempt.
post.summary: Compute costing for a work order

get.description: Returns the costing record together with all of its adjustments and the running effective totals (record + Σ deltas). Used by the accountant adjustment dialog so the FE never has to do money arithmetic itself.
get.summary: Get costing record + adjustments + effective totals

post.summary: Finalize costing for a work order

parameters.description: filter by customer id (uuid); omit for all customers
parameters.name: customer_id

post.description: Bridges a customer-facing SKU code (as it appears in the customer's packing-list Excel) with the internal catalog SKU id. (#304, BR-CSM02)

post.description: Multipart upload with form fields `customer_id` (uuid) and `file` (CSV, UTF-8 with optional BOM). CSV header: `customer_sku_code,sku_id,notes`. Fail-all: any row error rolls the whole batch back and returns 422 with per-row errors.
post.summary: Bulk-import customer SKU mappings from CSV

patch.description: Partial update; nil fields leave the column untouched. The (customer_id, customer_sku_code) PK is immutable — to rename the customer code, delete and re-create.

get.description: Returns an A4 PDF listing every ALLOCATED remnant for the work order, grouped by storage zone for efficient walking order. Returns 404 when no remnants are allocated.
get.summary: Generate pick-slip PDF for a work order

get.description:
Cross-container queue used by the planner dashboard. Filters
by status (pending|approved|rejected|all), container_id,
customer_id, exception_type, and a created_at date range.
get.summary: List loading exceptions across containers (#328 — keyset paginated)

post.description:
Partial-success response: every id lands in approved or failed
with a structured code (NOT_FOUND / INVALID_TRANSITION /
INVALID_INPUT / PRECONDITION_FAILED / INTERNAL). Resolutions
BACKORDER and SUBSTITUTE_ACCEPTED are rejected — they require
per-row context.
post.summary: Approve up to 50 loading exceptions in one batch (#330)

get.summary: Pinned counter — pending exceptions + blocked containers (#328)
Get one loading exception by id

patch.description:
Resolution must be one of BACKORDER / CANCEL_FROM_SO /
SUBSTITUTE_ACCEPTED / WRITE_OFF / DEFER_TO_NEXT.
BR-D17 BACKORDER: parent_so_line_id is required and a
carry-over sales_order_lines row is created in the same tx.
BR-D18 SUBSTITUTE_ACCEPTED: substitute_sku_id is required.

Closes the exception without picking a resolution.
resolution column stays NULL but approved_by/approved_at are stamped
so the SEAL guard treats it as resolved.

get.summary: Get one loading plan with its lines

post.description:
When the container already has scanned container_lines, the
caller MUST set confirm_supersede=true. Without it the
endpoint returns 412 so the FE can render the confirm dialog.

parameters.name: status

parameters.name: date

parameters.name: preset

parameters.name: assigned

post.description:
Creates a PLANNED work order. If the SKU has dimensions and there
is at least one fitting remnant in stock, the system writes a
REMNANT_BYPASSED row to inventory_audit_log to record that the
planner did not allocate any of the suggestions (BR-K05). Provide
`bypass_reason` to attach a free-text note to that audit row.
post.summary: Create work order

get.summary: List work orders assigned to the authenticated CNC operator

post.description:
Transitions a work order. When advancing PLANNED→IN_CUTTING with `sheet_id`,
the inventory module pre-assigns the sheet and rejects the call with 412 if
remnant overflow is RED (≥15% of total stock). Admin callers may force the
bypass by setting `bypass_overflow=true` together with a non-empty
`bypass_reason`; the bypass is recorded in `inventory_audit_log` with
action=OVERFLOW_BYPASSED.
post.summary: Advance work order status

412.description: precondition failed (operator mismatch, costing missing, remnant overflow lock, non-admin bypass)

post.summary: Assign a PLANNED work order to a CNC operator
Assign a machine shift slot to a work order

get.summary: List consumption records for work order

post.summary: Record material consumption for work order
Set estimated hours for scheduling

get.summary: List labor cost entries for a work order

post.description: Adds one labor cost line (stage, minutes, hourly rate). Cost contribution is `minutes * rate_per_hour / 60` and is aggregated by the costing module at compute-cost time. Rejected with 409 when the WO's costing record is finalized.
post.summary: Record a per-stage labor cost entry for a work order

post.description:
Closes the WO with an actual_qty < quantity and optionally
spawns a carry-over WO for the shortfall (#292). Status flips
IN_PROCESSING → PARTIAL_COMPLETE; the carry-over (when
requested) starts PLANNED with parent_wo_id pointing at the
parent. Sales-order line, sku, and plan are inherited so SO
traceability is preserved.
post.summary: Partial-complete a work order

400.description: actual_qty out of range or shortfall_reason invalid

post.summary: Suggest the least-busy CNC operator for a work order

get.summary: Suggest available machine shift slots for a work order

post.summary: Remove machine slot assignment from a work order

lines.description:
Computed projections — populated by GetContainer; List does not hydrate
these to keep the page query a single round-trip.

source_line.description: nil when the source line was fully consumed

bounding_box_length_mm.description:
BoundingBoxLengthMM and BoundingBoxWidthMM define the usable area of the
new remnant produced by this cut (e.g. after a chipped corner is excluded).
Both must be provided together. If omitted, the system defaults to the
actual remnant dimension so that search queries always have a value to
filter on. Must not exceed the corresponding RemnantDimension axis.

is_waste.description:
IsWaste signals that the leftover area is intentionally discarded
(BR-K02). Exactly one of RemnantDimension or IsWaste must indicate
the leftover outcome — leaving both nil/false is rejected so that
implicit waste cannot slip through.

bypass_reason.description:
BypassReason is an optional planner note recorded on the REMNANT_BYPASSED
audit row when the work order is created without allocating any of the
fitting remnant suggestions (BR-K05).

actual_qty.description:
ActualQty is the produced count when status=PARTIAL_COMPLETE (#292).
Nil for any other status. Always <= Quantity (chk_actual_qty).

parent_wo_id.description:
ParentWOID, when set, identifies the WO this one carried over from.
Set on auto-spawned carry-over WOs; nil otherwise.

sales_order_line_id.description:
SalesOrderLineID, when set, links the WO back to a sales_order_lines row
(Phase A pivot). Nullable so legacy/PO-rooted WOs read fine without it.

shortfall_reason.description:
ShortfallReason explains why the WO came up short. One of
MATERIAL_SHORTAGE | DEFECT | TIME_SHORTAGE | OTHER. Set together with
ActualQty; both nil for full COMPLETED transitions.
